---
name: Position Statement template
about: Submit a position statement for the E-commerce for Humans and AI Agents Workshop.

labels: ''

---

Thank you for submitting a position statement for the **E-commerce for Humans and AI Agents Workshop**.

Information submitted through GitHub will be public. If you do not want your submission to be public, please submit it by email to group-ecommerce-agents-pc@w3.org instead.

A position statement is intended for participants who would like to share a perspective, use case, technical proposal, implementation experience, research finding, standardization gap, or discussion topic with the workshop.

It should briefly explain **what you would like to contribute**, **why it is relevant to the workshop**, and **how it can help shape the workshop discussion or outcomes**.

Suggested length: **500-1000 words**, maximum **2 pages**.

## Title

<!-- Title of your proposed contribution or position statement -->

## Name

<!-- Your full name -->

## Affiliation

<!-- Organization, company, institution, or independent -->

## Email

<!-- Contact email address -->

## Preferred contribution format

<!-- Please keep one or more options that apply. -->

- Presentation
- Panel contribution
- Breakout discussion topic
- Demonstration
- Written input only
- Other:

## Expected attendance

<!-- Please keep one option. This helps the organizers plan venue capacity and remote participation support. -->

- In person
- Remote
- Either
- Not sure yet

## Summary of proposed contribution

<!-- Briefly summarize what you would like to share with the workshop. -->

## Relevance to the workshop

<!-- Explain how your proposed contribution relates to e-commerce for humans and AI agents.

For example, you may discuss AI agents in shopping, product discovery, search, recommendation, negotiation, checkout, payment, delivery, customer support, trust, safety, accessibility, or related Web technologies.
-->

## Main points or position

<!-- What are the main arguments, observations, lessons, concerns, or proposals you would like the workshop to consider? -->

## Use cases, examples, or evidence

<!-- Please describe any concrete use cases, scenarios, deployments, user needs, research findings, or implementation experience that support your contribution. -->

## Relationship to Web standards or technologies

<!-- Which Web standards, vocabularies, protocols, or technologies are relevant to your contribution?

For example: schema.org, GS1 Web Vocabulary, JSON-LD, MCP, UCP, ACP, Web Payments, Web Authentication, Verifiable Credentials, privacy, security, accessibility, browser technologies, search, content discovery, or agent protocols.
-->

## Questions for workshop discussion

<!-- What questions would you like the workshop participants to discuss based on your contribution? -->

## Suggested outcome

<!-- What outcome would you like this contribution to support?

For example: workshop report, best practices, standards gap analysis, use cases, Community Group proposal, liaison work, implementation experiments, or future standardization work.
-->

## Publication

May we publish your statement on the workshop website?

<!-- Please keep one option. -->

- Yes
- No
- Contact me first
